commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
fix: v28 goal_r curriculum + entropy bug fixes

Two bugs that caused training collapse after iter 500:

1. goal_r expansion used wrong signal (stag_ema instead of goal_fine)
   - stag_ema<0.25 = no stagnation TERMINATIONS, NOT robot reaching goals
   - Robot dodged 5s/1m stagnation check while barely moving (anti_stag=-24.975)
   - Result: goal_r expanded 1.2m→2.4m while robot was actually stuck
   - Fix: require goal_fine_avg>3.0 AND stag_ema<0.25 before expanding
   - Also: cooldown 200→400 iters (slower expansion)

2. entropy_coef was overridden to 0.004 every iter in update_curricula
   - Our noise-collapse fix (entropy_coef=0.02 in PPO config) was cancelled each iter
   - Fix: set runner.alg.entropy_coef=0.02 (fixed, no decay)

Also: start _goal_radius at 1.2m (proven working) instead of 1.0m

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Mature validation suite: I-14 adaptive quorum, Exp 15, adversarial tests, cross-seed stability

Seven hardening items implemented:

1. I-14 Adaptive Quorum Tolerance: adaptive_quorum(f) = 0.50 - 0.30*f
   added to shared/config.py. Dedicated test_adaptive_quorum() in Exp 10
   validates formula correctness, monotonicity, and sensitivity gain at
   high prune fractions vs fixed quorum (500 trials, Wilson CI).

2. Experiment 15 — SMILE-Gated Ratchet (5 tests):
   - SMILE vs MINE gate decision agreement (10 seeds, >=90% threshold)
   - SMILE estimation stability (CV < 0.15)
   - NaN safety across dims 2-64
   - Tighter bound verification at d=32, d=64
   - Boundary decision agreement near rho threshold

3. Adversarial tests added to Exp 14 (3 tests):
   - NaN resilience: cascade rejects gracefully on NaN predictions
   - Total prune rejection: pruning all experts caught before CoW
   - Empty prune acceptance: no-op prune passes trivially

4. Cross-seed stability test in Exp 14:
   - Same cascade scenario across 10 random seeds
   - PASS if >=8/10 produce consistent outcome

5. Seed pinning fixes: Exp 12 main() now sets both torch and numpy seeds

6. Paper-code alignment: I-14 formula now implemented in code, not just paper

Suite now: 15 experiments, 21 source files, all passing syntax check.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix audio playback stopping early on fMP4 recordings

MediaRecorder writes duration=0 in the moov header of fragmented
MP4 files, causing browsers to stop playback prematurely. Fix by
fetching the full audio file as a blob and creating a blob URL,
which lets the browser determine the real duration from the
actual data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Auto-link ticker symbols and company names to stock pages

summary, verdict, claims) now automatically link to their
/stock/[ticker] pages. Helps internal linking and user navigation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: usage dashboard — model name, cost split, token budget (#15)

* fix: disable cron jobs on trial expiry/payment lapse, re-enable on reactivation

Three-layer fix for cron messages leaking to suspended tenants:

1. CronDeliveryView: blocks delivery for non-ACTIVE tenants (returns 200
   to prevent QStash retries). Belt-and-suspenders defense.

2. expire_trials + handle_invoice_payment_failed: disable all cron jobs
   via gateway BEFORE hibernating container. Jobs are disabled (not
   deleted) so they can be restored on reactivation.

3. handle_checkout_completed: re-enables all disabled cron jobs when a
   suspended tenant subscribes and their container wakes up.

New module: apps/cron/suspension.py
- suspend_tenant_crons(): disables all enabled cron jobs
- resume_tenant_crons(): re-enables all disabled cron jobs
- Both are idempotent and handle partial failures gracefully

12 new tests covering suspend/resume/block lifecycle.

* fix: update suspended tenant tests to match 'paused' copy

Tests were asserting 'trial has ended' but the user-facing copy was
changed to 'assistant is paused' in a573951. Aligns test assertions
with current copy.

* fix: use web_fetch instead of curl for weather in briefing prompt

Subscriber agents have group:web (web_fetch, web_search) but NOT exec.
The morning briefing prompt was telling them to use 'curl' which they
can't run — causing hallucinated weather data.

Now explicitly instructs: use web_fetch tool, do NOT use curl/exec.

* fix: switch weather source to Open-Meteo API (web_fetch compatible)

wttr.in via web_fetch is unreliable — JSON format fails, special char
formats fail, 15-25s latency on simple queries.

Open-Meteo works perfectly: structured JSON, 3s response, free, no auth.
Prompt now includes WMO weather code legend so agent can interpret.

* feat: template weather URL with tenant coordinates into briefing prompt

Instead of telling the agent to 'build the URL with the user's
coordinates' (which it can't do without knowing them), we now:

1. Map IANA timezone → approximate lat/lon (major city per timezone)
2. Build the full Open-Meteo URL server-side
3. Template it directly into the morning briefing prompt

The agent just needs to call web_fetch with the pre-built URL.
No guessing coordinates, no hallucinating weather data.

New module: apps/orchestrator/weather.py
- 50+ timezone→coords mappings
- build_weather_url(tz) → ready-to-fetch URL
- get_coords_for_timezone(tz) with region-based fallbacks

* feat: add user location fields for accurate weather

Simplest-first approach:

1. User model: added location_city, location_lat, location_lon fields
2. Runtime profile endpoint: accepts location fields via PATCH
3. Plugin tool (nbhd_update_profile): added location params
4. Weather URL builder: prefers stored coords, falls back to timezone
5. AGENTS.md template: tells agent to ask for city if not set

Flow: agent asks user their city → looks up coords via web_search →
calls nbhd_update_profile → config refreshes → next morning briefing
uses real coordinates.

Migration: 0022_add_user_location_fields

* feat: full location stack — serializer, settings UI, runtime endpoint, onboarding, tests

Complete location feature buildout:

Backend:
- UserSerializer: exposes location_city, location_lat, location_lon
- ProfileView (user-facing PATCH): accepts location fields, triggers
  config refresh on change
- RuntimeProfileUpdateView (agent-facing PATCH): accepts location fields
  with coordinate validation (-90..90 lat, -180..180 lon)
- Onboarding USER.md: includes location if set

Frontend:
- Settings page: Location card with city input, edit/save/cancel flow
- API client: updated updateProfile type signature
- Copy: 'Not set — used for weather and local info' placeholder

Agent:
- AGENTS.md template: tells agent to ask for city if not set, look up
  coords via web_search, call nbhd_update_profile
- Plugin tool (nbhd_update_profile): accepts location_city, location_lat,
  location_lon params

Tests:
- 4 ProfileLocationAPITest: GET includes fields, PATCH city, PATCH with
  coords, config refresh on change
- 4 RuntimeProfileLocationTest: set city, set with coords, invalid
  coords 400, response includes fields

* fix: add location fields to AuthUser type + polish

- Add location_city, location_lat, location_lon to AuthUser interface
  (fixes frontend-test CI failure)
- Add null guard, autoFocus, maxLength to location input
- Add lat/lon range validators to UserSerializer

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add missing openclaw.plugin.json for nbhd-usage-reporter

Container was failing to start cleanly because OpenClaw couldn't find
the plugin manifest. The plugin had package.json with an 'openclaw' key
but was missing the separate openclaw.plugin.json file that OpenClaw
actually looks for. All other plugins had this file.

This is what's causing the 'Could not load scheduled tasks' timeout —
invalid config puts the gateway in degraded mode.

* fix: usage dashboard — model name, cost split, token budget

- Add minimax/minimax-m2.5 alias to MODEL_RATES so OpenClaw model names
  resolve to "MiniMax M2.5" instead of "Unknown Model"
- Replace misleading subscription-minus-AI formula with fixed $4.75
  infra estimate in "Where Your Money Goes"
- Raise monthly_token_budget default from 500k to 2M (with data migration)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: add Biome linter + Lefthook pre-commit hooks (#28)

* chore: add Biome linter + Lefthook pre-commit hooks

Add fast linting and formatting guardrails to make the repo more
AI-coding-friendly and reduce errors from code generation tools.

Setup:
- Biome (Rust-based linter+formatter): 75 files in ~30ms
- Lefthook pre-commit hook: auto-lint staged files on commit
- Claude Code PostToolUse hook: auto-lint on every Edit/Write
- Deny --no-verify in .claude/settings.json

Key rules enabled: noUnusedImports, noUnusedVariables, useConst,
useImportType, noDoubleEquals, noVar, useHookAtTopLevel.

Code fixes:
- Timeline.tsx: move early return after hooks (rules of hooks)
- Fix unused imports, implicit any, unused params
- Format all source files with consistent style

feat: production-grade AWS executor with deep QA fixes

Deepen ECS, ALB, Lambda, RDS, and ElastiCache handlers with full
lifecycle support (multi-step CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE), cross-cutting
concerns (CloudWatch alarms with resource-scoped dimensions, log
retention), and comprehensive input validation.

Key changes:
- ECS: full cluster→task def→service lifecycle with buildECSTaskDef
  helper, deterministic env var ordering, partial result on failure
- ALB: full LB→target group→listener lifecycle with cascade delete,
  fallback to DescribeListeners/DescribeTargetGroups for state evolution
- Lambda: extracted lambdaVpcConfig helper, VPC + layers support
- alarm_on: target allowlist, resource-scoped Dimensions, sanitized errors
- Validation: ECS cpu/memory, ALB ports, RDS monitoring_interval + role_arn,
  ElastiCache az_mode, alarm_on syntax
- Security: added database_url/connection_url/dsn to sensitive keys

QA fixes (19 findings across data flow, error handling, security, DRY):
- Return partial ApplyResult on multi-step CREATE failure (orphaned resources)
- ECS UPDATE handles desired_count independently of image_uri
- ECS DELETE surfaces scale-down errors instead of swallowing
- ALB UPDATE supports SetSecurityGroups/SetSubnets
- logRetentionDays guards against zero/negative input

commit.message:
feat: staged launch state transition + warn-only staleness check (#327)

* feat: staged launch — state transition + warn-only staleness check (#322 #323)

- SessionExecutionBridge.prepareCycle() now calls updateCycleState() to
  transition the cycle from planning → active after preparing all runs.
  updateCycleState() reads the cycle JSON, sets state = 'active', and
  writes it back via JsonStore.

- New staleness-check module: checkBetsForIssueRefs() scans bet descriptions
  for #N issue refs and STALE_SIGNAL_PATTERN ("closes/fixes/resolves #N").
  formatStalenessWarnings() renders human-readable CLI output.

- staged launch staleness behavior: warn on any #N refs but only BLOCK on
  likelyStale (explicit closes/fixes/resolves language). Plain tracking refs
  like "(#322)" proceed after warning. --force skips all checks.
  --block-on-refs opts in to strict blocking on any ref.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address coderabbitai review issues on staged launch (#327)

- session-bridge.ts: import CycleState type (fixes build failure)
- session-bridge.ts: use cycle.id (resolved UUID) not raw cycleId param
- cycle.ts: remove duplicate manager.updateState() call — bridge.prepareCycle() already transitions state
- staleness-check.ts: add comment clarifying prose-only scope of checkBetsForIssueRefs
- staleness-check.test.ts: use Vitest globals, remove explicit import

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Prevent invalid transitions (e.g. complete → active) by checking
ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS before writing. Logs a warning and returns early
on disallowed transitions.

feat: Capped Self-Optimizing Worklist — hard cap 15 symbols with displacement

Trade data proves top 15 symbols PF=3.76 (+$1,205) vs all 192 symbols PF=0.59
(-$3,671). This commit enforces a hard cap with intelligent displacement:

- Hard cap at 15 active/candidate symbols in worklist pipeline
- Displacement: new symbol must beat lowest scorer to enter at cap
- get_lowest_scorer(), remove(), active_count() on WorklistStore
- rescore_and_trim() with time-decay (10+ min stale = score penalty)
- Periodic trim loop every 5 min (removes below-threshold + over-cap)
- Force-add uses full pipeline (no more score=100 bypass)
- Persistent watchlist save/load respects cap
- WORKLIST_DISPLACED event type for observability
- 8 tests (all passing, 592 existing tests unaffected)

commit.message:
rewrite pi_leds for SK9822/APA102 addressable LEDs via hardware SPI

Switches from simple GPIO HIGH/LOW (one pin per row) to APA102 protocol
over hardware SPI on GPIO 10 (MOSI/data) and GPIO 11 (SCLK/clock).
90 LEDs, 3 per grid slot (3 rows x 10 cols). Color and per-slot
highlighting now work on real hardware. Requires: pip install apa102-pi

Uses jsPDF with autotable to generate landscape PDF with header, summary, district-wise table, and member details with colored status.

feat: Add iOS Tier 1 snapshot gaps (5 tests + 5 reference PNGs) (#906)

Add Loading/NotFound/Empty snapshot tests for 3 iOS screens:
DeviceDetail (Loading, NotFound), EditDevice (Loading, NotFound),
and HistoryList (Empty). Update parity matrix to reflect new
coverage (35 → 40 PNGs). Remaining iOS Tier 1 gaps are blocked
on screen implementation (DeviceTypeDetail, EditBatteryEvent).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(parser): implement complete public API with Deno backend integration

- Implement parse_source() and parse_file() synchronous APIs with tokio runtime
- Implement parse_source_async() and parse_file_async() async APIs
- Add from_json() method to AstArena for JSON AST deserialization
- Add RuntimeError variant to ParseError for runtime failures
- Update public API tests with comprehensive coverage for all parse functions
- Add new dependencies: tokio, async-trait, fxhash, hashbrown, lasso, rustc-hash
- Update .gitignore to exclude .planning directory properly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: stateless agent execution via immediate compression and sliding window (#54)

Replace the O(n) per-step compression scan in all 4 agent tool-loops
(implementer, debugger, security, archivist) with two mechanisms:

1. Immediate compression at insertion time — tool results are compressed
   via compress_tool_result() before entering the message history, and
   write_file arguments are compressed inline via compress_write_file_args().

2. Sliding-window pruning — when messages exceed a threshold (16 for
   30-step agents, 12-14 for shorter ones), old messages are dropped and
   replaced with a structured checkpoint summary (no extra LLM call).

Also compacts previous_fixes in the debug retry loop to store only
essential fields (~500 chars) instead of full debug results.

Shared compression logic is extracted into context_compressor.py,
eliminating ~120 lines of duplicated code across the 4 agents.

Expected impact: ~60-78% reduction in prompt tokens for long agent
loops, O(1) compression cost per step, stable prompt sizes in debug
retry loops.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01WuS5ndU73aJt2Gwh5yw5iC

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: glitchlab
repository.description: An agentic development engine for repo-agnostic, multi-agent code evolution under strict governance. Build Weird. Ship Clean.

commit.message:
feat(ui): polish styling for professional look (#31)

- Add Inter font with antialiasing for cleaner typography
- Softer user message bubbles (light tint instead of solid primary)
- Branded assistant avatar with indigo tint
- Message entrance animations (fade-in + slide-up)
- Improved dark mode contrast (deeper background, more card separation)
- Wider sidebar (w-72), folder/chat icons, active session highlight
- Larger header fonts (text-base), increased message spacing (px-6 py-5)
- PromptInput focus glow (shadow-md + ring on focus-within)
- Thicker progress bar with gradient
- PlanPanel redesign: card container, larger fonts, Badge progress counter
- Home view Sparkles icon above title
- Markdown body font bumped to 15px for readability
- Thinking indicator: larger dots, no text, branded avatar
- Fix missing `sessions` dependency in Sidebar useCallback

Co-authored-by: Suman Addanki <suman@Addanki-Mac.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Add comprehensive error handling to all pages

This fixes the 'Application error: server-side exception' during Vercel deployment

Changes:
- Add try-catch blocks to all data fetching functions
- Check response.ok before parsing JSON
- Return safe defaults (empty arrays/objects) on errors
- Add optional chaining for all potentially undefined values
- Fix stats calculation to handle null/undefined values
- Ensure all pages render correctly with or without database

Pages fixed:
- app/page.tsx (homepage)
- app/news/page.tsx (news listing)
- app/news/[slug]/page.tsx (news detail)
- app/products/page.tsx (products listing)
- app/products/[slug]/page.tsx (product detail)
- lib/news-db.js (news database operations)
- app/api/stats/route.js (stats API)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
